name: sim_weyl_group_a2_root_system
scope_note: Shell-local probe: Weyl group W(A2) = S3 (order 6) and A2 root system. Tests: group order, root permutation, orthogonality, braid relation, Cartan matrix, Clifford reflections, Cayley graph, XGI hyperedges. z3 UNSAT: Weyl element cannot change root length.

pytorch.reason: Represent A2 roots as 2D tensors; apply all 6 Weyl group elements as 2x2 orthogonal matrices; verify permutation closure on the 6-root set and preservation of root lengths

pyg.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

z3.reason: UNSAT: Weyl group element w AND |w(alpha1)|^2 != |alpha1|^2; Weyl group preserves root lengths — violation is structurally impossible

cvc5.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

sympy.reason: Symbolic Cartan matrix A2; compute simple reflections s_i(lambda) = lambda - <lambda, alpha_i^v> * alpha_i; verify braid relation s1*s2*s1 = s2*s1*s2 symbolically

clifford.reason: A2 roots as grade-1 elements in Cl(2,0); reflection across hyperplane perpendicular to root alpha: s_alpha(v) = -alpha*v*alpha^{-1}; verify Clifford reflection maps roots to roots

geomstats.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

e3nn.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

rustworkx.reason: Cayley graph of W(A2) with generators {s1, s2}: 6 nodes, edges from s1 and s2 actions; verify graph has correct node/edge count for Dih3 Cayley graph

xgi.reason: Hyperedge {s1_idx, s2_idx, w0_idx} representing the longest element as product of generators; hyperedge {alpha1, alpha2, -(alpha1+alpha2)} as the simple-root negative-sum triple

toponetx.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

gudhi.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry shell-local probe; deferred to pairwise coupling

P1_pytorch_weyl_group_order_6.reason: W(A2) = S3 has order 6; generated by {s1, s2} with braid+order-2 relations

P2_pytorch_orbit_of_alpha1_is_all_6_root.reason: W(A2) acts transitively on the 6-root set; orbit of alpha1 = all 6 roots

P3_pytorch_weyl_elements_orthogonal.reason: All W(A2) elements are orthogonal transformations: M^T M = I, det = +/-1

P4_pytorch_weyl_group_permutes_roots.reason: Every W(A2) element maps each root to another root in the 6-root set

P5_sympy_braid_relation_s1s2s1_eq_s2s1s2.reason: A2 braid relation s1*s2*s1 = s2*s1*s2 verified symbolically (Coxeter relation for A2)

P6_sympy_cartan_matrix_a2.reason: A2 Cartan matrix = [[2,-1],[-1,2]]; off-diagonal -1 encodes 120-degree angle between simple roots

P7_clifford_reflections_map_roots_to_roo.reason: Clifford reflection -alpha*v*alpha^{-1} maps each A2 root to another A2 root

P8_rustworkx_cayley_graph_6_nodes.reason: Cayley graph of W(A2) = S3 with generators {s1,s2}: 6 nodes, 12 directed edges (2 per node)

P9_xgi_hyperedges_encode_weyl_constraint.reason: XGI hyperedges: {s1,s2,w0} and {alpha1,alpha2,-(alpha1+alpha2)} encode A2 Weyl structure

N1_pytorch_order_not_5.reason: W(A2) has exactly 6 elements, not 5; S3 has no subgroup of order 5

N2_pytorch_simple_reflections_not_identi.reason: Simple reflections s1, s2 are NOT the identity; they flip signs along their respective root hyperplanes

N3_z3_unsat_weyl_changes_root_length.reason: UNSAT: orthogonal matrix AND |w(alpha1)|^2 != 1 is a direct contradiction; Weyl preserves lengths

N4_sympy_s1s2_squared_not_identity.reason: (s1*s2)^2 != I for A2; the Coxeter order is 3, not 2 (A2 is NOT A1 x A1)

N5_clifford_reflection_no_grade2_leakage.reason: Clifford reflection of grade-1 vector stays grade-1; no grade-2 (bivector) component created

B1_pytorch_longest_element_maps_positive.reason: Longest element w0 = s1*s2*s1 maps all positive A2 roots to negative roots

B2_pytorch_longest_element_order_2.reason: w0^2 = I in W(A2): the longest element is an involution (order 2)

B3_sympy_coxeter_relation_s1s2_order_3.reason: (s1*s2)^3 = I for A2; Coxeter order of the pair (s1,s2) is exactly 3

B4_rustworkx_cayley_graph_connected.reason: Cayley graph of W(A2) is strongly connected: all 6 elements reachable from identity via s1, s2

B5_xgi_positive_root_triple_hyperedge.reason: XGI: positive root triple {alpha1, alpha2, alpha1+alpha2} forms a 3-way hyperedge

B6_z3_sat_identity_preserves_roots.reason: SAT: identity element preserves all root lengths; consistent encoding
